priorities: Long‑term wealth transmission to children and family
Preserving and expanding large‑cap healthcare/pharma holdings, especially those focused on neuro‑degenerative research
Maintaining institutional stability and rigorous, data‑driven execution of the Global Balanced Growth mandate
Ensuring sufficient liquidity for corporate cash flow and philanthropic grants
Leveraging wealth as a weapon to fund neuroscience breakthroughs for his daughter
redLines: Divest from any pharma/healthcare company that reduces, abandons or defunds Parkinson's (or broader neuro‑degenerative) research
Accept no investment that prioritises cheap lifestyle profits over serious R&D in brain disease
Any deviation from a rigorous, data‑driven execution of the balanced mandate
preferredSectors: Large‑cap pharmaceuticals & healthcare (with strong neuroscience R&D)
avoidedSectors: Companies that do not support neuro‑degenerative research
High‑volatility speculative sectors (e.g., crypto, early‑stage tech without clear R&D focus)
Lifestyle‑oriented consumer firms that chase cheap profits
esgFocus: Philanthropic impact on brain disease breakthroughs

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Multi‑generational, with short‑term liquidity for philanthropic grants but overall long‑term wealth preservation
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Moderate – needs cash for corporate liquidity, family expenses and foundation grants while keeping core holdings stable

lifeEvents.description: Daughter Chloe was reported to be studying abroad and doing well.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: No immediate change; reinforced focus on long‑term family wealth planning.

lifeEvents.description: In‑depth discussion of long‑term wealth transmission strategies for the children, with a 12% allocation to global healthcare giants.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Confirmed commitment to healthcare exposure and inter‑generational asset structuring.

lifeEvents.description: Chloe diagnosed with rapidly progressing early‑onset Parkinson’s disease; Hubertus expressed that his business now feels secondary.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Triggered a strategic pivot toward aggressive support of neuro‑degenerative research and related holdings.

lifeEvents.description: Family established a Venture Philanthropy foundation dedicated to funding neuro‑degenerative research; explicit mandate to use private wealth as a weapon to save Chloe.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reallocation of capital toward pharma/biotech firms with strong Parkinson’s pipelines and direct foundation grants.

lifeEvents.description: CHF 1 million transferred from the private portfolio into the family foundation for an immediate grant to a neuro‑genetics lab in Geneva.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity moved from core holdings to impact‑driven grantmaking; reinforced focus on research‑centric assets.

lifeEvents.description: Hubertus met a biotech firm with a breakthrough Parkinson’s asset and reiterated that large‑cap holdings should act as primary financial support while his heart remains with neuroscience breakthroughs.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Potential tilt toward selective large‑cap biotech exposure and active monitoring of R&D commitments.

personalProfile.interests: automotive industry, high‑impact neuroscience research, art sponsorship, family gatherings
personalProfile.quirks: frequently mentions his daughter’s health as the central motivator; emotionally driven when discussing Parkinson’s
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: discussion of cutting‑edge brain disease research, updates on Chloe’s condition, art and cultural events, data‑driven performance reviews
personalProfile.avoidTopics: competitor banks, lifestyle‑only profit chasing, sectors that ignore R&D

keyQuotes: My automotive business means nothing if I cannot save my daughter. Everything has changed.
Our private wealth is now a weapon to save Chloe. I want our core healthcare and pharma holdings to actively support the entities pushing the boundaries of brain disease research.
If a company we own ever abandons or defunds Parkinson's research to chase cheap lifestyle profits, it would feel like a personal betrayal of my daughter. Flag them immediately for divestment.
Seeing these brilliant researchers is our only hope. We must keep our capital flowing to the companies leading the fight against this disease.
I expect rigorous, data‑driven execution of my balanced mandate.
